labs.name: Dixon Lab
labs.overview: The lab studies protein phosphatases, focusing on their roles in cellular signaling, cancer, neuronal development, and bacterial pathogenesis.

labs.name: Paul Lab
labs.overview: The Paul Lab studies fatty acid metabolism in trypanosomes, focusing on understanding the molecular and cellular mechanisms of these single-celled eukaryotic pathogens.

labs.name: Sicklick Lab
labs.overview: The lab investigates mechanisms of gastrointestinal stromal tumor (GIST) tumorigenesis, disease persistence, and drug resistance, with a focus on identifying novel therapeutic agents for curing GIST.

labs.name: Broide Lab
labs.overview: The Broide Lab focuses on understanding the role of airway inflammation in contributing to asthma and airway remodeling, with studies on eosinophils and innate immune mechanisms.

labs.name: Theodorakis Research Group
labs.overview: The research group focuses on synthetic chemistry, natural products synthesis, drug discovery, and developing fluorescent probes for imaging and sensing.

labs.name: Division of Pulmonary, Critical Care, Sleep Medicine & Physiology
labs.overview: A division dedicated to providing state-of-the-art diagnosis and management of respiratory, critical care, and sleep medicine conditions while conducting innovative research.

labs.name: Cohen Research Group
labs.overview: The research group performs studies in inorganic, bioinorganic, medicinal, materials, and supramolecular chemistry, with current focus areas including metal-organic frameworks, metalloenzyme inhibitors, and metallofragments.

labs.name: Debelouchina Lab
labs.overview: The lab uses chemical biology and structural biology tools to study molecular mechanisms of gene silencing, aging-related diseases, and the connections between gene regulation and neurodegenerative disease.

labs.name: Devaraj Research Group
labs.overview: The lab focuses on understanding how non-living matter can assemble to form life, developing artificial cells, and creating chemical tools to study cellular structures and functions.

labs.name: Loomba Lab
labs.overview: The Loomba Lab focuses on research in Metabolic Dysfunction Associated Steatotic Liver Disease (MASLD), previously known as non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD).

labs.name: Wettersten Lab
labs.overview: The lab explores new therapeutic approaches to stop cancer progression using animal models, with a particular interest in understanding and targeting communication between cancer cells and the tumor microenvironment.

labs.name: Computational Social Science
labs.overview: Computational Social Science brings together an interdisciplinary community spanning the School of Social Sciences to create a thriving ecosystem of discovery, training, and impact.

labs.name: Program in Materials Science and Engineering
labs.overview: The Program in Materials Science and Engineering at UC San Diego is an interdisciplinary program focused on advanced materials research and education across multiple departments.

labs.name: Scripps Institution of Oceanography
labs.overview: A research institution dedicated to understanding and protecting the planet through oceanographic, earth science, and climate research across multiple disciplines.

labs.name: Scripps Institution of Oceanography
labs.overview: A research institution dedicated to understanding and protecting the planet through oceanographic, earth science, and climate research across multiple disciplines.

labs.name: Hamdoun Lab
labs.overview: The Hamdoun Lab studies marine biology, with a focus on sea urchins and cellular research at the Scripps Institution of Oceanography.

labs.name: Gutkind Lab
labs.overview: The Gutkind Lab focuses on studying G proteins, GPCRs, and signaling pathways in cancer, with an emphasis on developing precision prevention and treatment strategies for head and neck cancer and other malignancies.

labs.name: Center for Academic Research and Training in Anthropogeny (CARTA)
labs.overview: CARTA is a virtual organization that explores and explains the origins of humans through transdisciplinary research, focusing on understanding human uniqueness compared to other hominids.

labs.name: Abagyan Lab
labs.overview: The Abagyan Lab focuses on developing technologies for structure-based drug discovery, structural systems biology, and target identification through protein modeling and computational screening.

labs.name: Program in Materials Science and Engineering
labs.overview: The Program in Materials Science and Engineering at UC San Diego is an interdisciplinary program focused on advanced materials research and education across multiple departments.

labs.name: School of Biological Sciences
labs.overview: The School of Biological Sciences at UC San Diego conducts research across four academic departments to advance knowledge of basic biological sciences and apply research discoveries to improve human health, protect the environment, and enrich the economy.

labs.name: Hammer Laboratory
labs.overview: The Hammer laboratory develops quantitative tools to analyze or mimic biology, with particular interest in cell adhesion, motility in the immune response, and developing synthetic tools to mimic biology like artificial cells (protocells).

labs.name: Dr. Michael Liss Research Lab
labs.overview: A prostate cancer research lab focused on microbiome studies, clinical trials, and precision medicine for prostate cancer diagnosis and treatment.

labs.name: School of Biological Sciences at UC San Diego
labs.overview: The School of Biological Sciences conducts research across multiple biological disciplines, including cell biology, ecology, molecular biology, and neurobiology.

labs.name: Glass Laboratory
labs.overview: The Glass Laboratory investigates mechanisms by which transcription factors regulate the development and function of macrophages, using genome-wide approaches and technologies like DNA sequencing.

labs.name: Patel Lab
labs.overview: The Patel Lab focuses on computational immunopathology methods to integrate spatial context into understanding the tumor immune microenvironment using advanced computational and imaging techniques.

labs.name: Hojun Li Lab
labs.overview: A basic science biomedical research group focused on understanding blood cell production, with the goal of developing better diagnostics and therapeutics for blood disorders and leukemia.

labs.name: Muscle Physiology Research Group
labs.overview: A research group studying muscle physiology, focusing on muscle aging, muscle architecture, force production, and using advanced imaging techniques like MRI and ultrasound.

labs.name: Firtel Lab
labs.overview: The laboratory focuses on signal transduction pathways controlling directional cell movement (chemotaxis) and morphogenesis in eukaryotic cells, using Dictyostelium as an experimental system.

labs.name: The Kiger Laboratory
labs.overview: The lab studies cellular remodeling and membrane regulation, focusing on how cells maintain integrity and compartmentalization through membrane trafficking processes and lipid regulation in immune and muscle cells.

labs.name: UC San Diego Department of Surgery
labs.overview: A comprehensive surgical department conducting research across multiple surgical specialties including oncology, transplantation, cardiovascular surgery, and more.

labs.name: Pokorski Lab
labs.overview: The Pokorski Lab develops novel materials for biomedical applications by combining chemistry, protein engineering, and materials science to create innovative biocomposite materials.

labs.name: Lo Lab
labs.overview: The Lo Lab conducts research in biomedical devices, bioimaging systems, and photonic/electronic devices with nanomaterials, focusing on innovative solutions for scientific and technological challenges.

labs.name: Burkart Laboratory
labs.overview: The Burkart Lab studies secondary metabolites and enzymatic megasynthases responsible for synthesizing fatty acids, polyketides, and nonribosomal peptides, with applications in drug discovery, renewable energy, and biomaterials.

labs.name: KomivesLab
labs.overview: Work in the Komives Lab is focused on understanding the parameters that govern protein-protein interactions mediated by non-globular proteins. The lab studies four main systems including thrombin-thrombomodulin interaction, NFkB signaling system, Cullin 5 E3 ligases, and the urokinase-uPAR interaction.

labs.name: Salk Institute for Biological Studies
labs.overview: The Salk Institute is an independent, nonprofit research institute focused on foundational, collaborative research addressing society's most pressing challenges, including cancer, Alzheimer's disease, and agricultural vulnerability.

labs.name: Tezcan Lab
labs.overview: A research lab exploring, expanding, and evolving the roles of metals in biology.

labs.name: Kavli Institute for Brain and Mind (KIBM)
labs.overview: KIBM is a research institute that supports interdisciplinary neuroscience research, aiming to further understanding of the brain and mind across different levels of organization.

labs.name: Gallo Lab
labs.overview: The Gallo Lab researches how the skin and its resident microbiome defends the body against infections and the environment, using techniques from biochemistry, immunology, microbiology, and genetics.

labs.name: Chang Lab
labs.overview: The lab studies cellular and molecular mechanisms underlying T lymphocyte fate specification, focusing on how lymphocytes produce both terminally differentiated effector cells and self-renewing memory cells during immune responses.

labs.name: Rana Lab
labs.overview: The Rana Lab studies the fundamental mechanisms of RNA biology and chemistry, integrating advanced AI and machine-learning modeling to uncover disease pathogenesis and translate new discoveries into innovative therapies.

labs.name: Center for Academic Research and Training in Anthropogeny (CARTA)
labs.overview: CARTA is a virtual organization that explores and explains the origins of humans through transdisciplinary research, focusing on understanding human uniqueness compared to other great apes.

labs.name: Kavanaugh Lab
labs.overview: The Kavanaugh lab conducts clinical research on rheumatic diseases including lupus, rheumatoid arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, scleroderma, gout, and psoriatic arthritis.

labs.name: Nieh Lab
labs.overview: Research focuses on how natural and man-made stressors affect the biology and behaviors of bees, studying communication, health, and cognitive abilities of honey bees, bumble bees, and stingless bees.

labs.name: Guo Lab
labs.overview: The Guo Lab researches post-transcriptional genomic changes in head and neck cancer, utilizing computational biology methods to understand alternative splicing events and their impact on cancer progression.

labs.name: Frazer Lab
labs.overview: The Frazer Lab studies human genomics, using induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) to investigate how genetic variations impact molecular and physiological phenotypes across different cell types.

labs.name: Looger Lab
labs.overview: Loren Looger uses protein engineering to create tools to study the brain, including neurotransmitter detectors, improved labels for in vivo imaging, designed receptors, and rewired neural circuits.

labs.name: Shu Chien-Gene Lay Department of Bioengineering
labs.overview: The department applies engineering principles to scientific discovery and technology innovation in bioengineering, with research focusing on multiscale bioengineering, tissue engineering, regenerative medicine, and systems biology.

labs.name: Fahy Lab
labs.overview: The Fahy Lab focuses on researching asthma mechanisms, investigating abnormal immune responses, pathologic mucus gel formation, and the heterogeneity of molecular mechanisms in asthma to develop more personalized treatments.

labs.name: Center on Gender Equity and Health
labs.overview: A research center focused on gender equity and health issues within the Division of Infectious Diseases and Global Public Health at UC San Diego.

labs.name: Kwon Lab
labs.overview: The Kwon Lab engineers nanoscale materials to create tools, diagnostics, and therapeutics for the central nervous system, focusing on targeted delivery and activity-based sensing in brain injuries.

labs.name: Cognitive Dynamics Lab
labs.overview: A research lab focused on using technology and mobile assessments to study mental health, cognitive functioning, and developing innovative interventions for mental health conditions.

labs.name: UC San Diego Department of Pediatrics
labs.overview: A premier department devoted to improving understanding of disease pathogenesis in order to better diagnose, treat and prevent diseases in infants and children through clinical and basic science research.

labs.name: Scripps Institution of Oceanography
labs.overview: A research institution dedicated to understanding and protecting the planet through oceanographic, earth science, and climate research across multiple disciplines.

labs.name: Joseph Lab
labs.overview: The Joseph Lab studies the molecular mechanisms of protein synthesis, translational control in the brain, viral translational control, and artificial cell development.

labs.name: Zengler Lab
labs.overview: Our lab studies the complex interactions of microorganisms. We use a community systems biology approach to understand genome organization, metabolism, and exchanges between microbes in their environment or host.

labs.name: Signer Laboratory
labs.overview: The Signer Lab researches blood-forming stem cells, focusing on protein homeostasis, stem cell maintenance, and understanding how cellular processes impact stem cell longevity and regenerative capabilities.

labs.name: Baird Lab
labs.overview: The Baird Lab develops mathematical models and computational simulations of human physiological responses to trauma, with the goal of creating high-fidelity healthcare simulation training tools.

labs.name: Hur Lab
labs.overview: The Hur Laboratory investigates how the immune system distinguishes self from non-self, using structural biology, biochemistry, and genomics to study RNA recognition and transcriptional regulation in the immune system.

labs.name: Department of Pharmacology
labs.overview: The Department of Pharmacology at UC San Diego focuses on research exploring how chemicals and therapeutic agents affect biological systems, with the goal of advancing precision medicine and developing targeted therapeutic interventions.

labs.name: Lukowski Lab
labs.overview: The Lukowski Lab focuses on discovering novel enzymes from natural product biosynthetic pathways and their applications as biocatalysts in medicinal chemistry, with research spanning enzyme families, cyanobacterial natural products, and host-associated microbiome chemical ecology.

labs.name: Tor Lab
labs.overview: The Tor Lab focuses on studying nucleic acids–ligand interactions, developing new emissive nucleosides and oligonucleotides, and advancing novel cellular delivery agents.

labs.name: Applied Sciences Division
labs.overview: The Applied Sciences Division is part of the UC San Diego Department of Radiation Medicine & Applied Sciences, focusing on interdisciplinary research and clinical applications in cancer care.

labs.name: Aguado iBiomaterials Research Group
labs.overview: The Aguado iBiomaterials Research Group develops precision biomaterials as tools to determine how sex, age, and ancestry impact health and disease at multiple length scales. Their current focus is investigating how sex chromosome linked genes modulate sex differences in cardiovascular disease progression and treatments.

labs.name: Cardiac Mechanics Research Group
labs.overview: The Cardiac Mechanics Research Group uses experimental and computational models to investigate the relationships between the structure of cardiac muscle and the electrical and mechanical function of the heart during ventricular remodeling, repair and arrhythmia.

labs.name: Institute for Genomic Medicine (IGM)
labs.overview: The IGM aims to discover new pathways in human health and disease and translate these discoveries to clinical and preventive medicine through multidisciplinary research integrating clinical phenotypes with genomic approaches.

labs.name: Steinmetz Lab
labs.overview: The Steinmetz Lab researches plant virus nanotechnology for human health and plant health applications, focusing on developing nanomaterials and biologics derived from plant viruses for cancer immunotherapy, drug delivery, and agricultural innovations.

labs.name: School of Biological Sciences at UC San Diego
labs.overview: The School of Biological Sciences conducts research across multiple biological disciplines, advancing knowledge and applying research discoveries to improve human health, protect the environment, and enrich the economy.

labs.name: Opella Lab
labs.overview: The lab focuses on NMR structural studies of proteins in membranes and supramolecular assemblies, with a particular interest in membrane proteins and developing NMR spectroscopy methods.

labs.name: Wodarz Lab
labs.overview: The Wodarz lab conducts interdisciplinary research using computational models of biomedical processes, focusing on cancer modeling, virus dynamics, and evolutionary theory.

labs.name: Parast Lab
labs.overview: The Parast Lab explores the pathophysiology of placenta-based pregnancy disorders, including preeclampsia, fetal growth restriction, and stillbirth using various model systems like transgenic mouse models, primary placental cells, and stem cell-based culture systems.

labs.name: Molinski Lab
labs.overview: The lab investigates marine natural products, focusing on their chemical structure, synthesis, and potential biomedical applications, particularly in cancer and fungal disease treatment.

labs.name: Sanchez-Roige Lab
labs.overview: The lab advances precision psychiatry by synthesizing genetics, genomics, and medical bioinformatics to elucidate the genetic basis of psychiatric disorders, with a particular focus on substance use disorders.

labs.name: Wallace Lab
labs.overview: The Wallace Lab researches human physical activity, evolution, and health, focusing on how humans evolved to move and the impacts of modern physical activity patterns on human health.

labs.name: Systems Biology Workgroup of the Psychiatric Genomics Consortium for PTSD
labs.overview: The Systems Biology workgroup aims to identify genes differentially expressed in controls and individuals with PTSD, using a multimodal approach to study the complex interplay between biological and phenotypic constructs.

labs.name: A.E. Allen Lab
labs.overview: The lab researches marine microbial oceanography, focusing on gene function, biochemical pathways, and ecological characteristics of marine microeukaryotic phytoplankton lineages.

labs.name: Saier Lab
labs.overview: The Saier Lab studies transcriptional and metabolic regulation in bacteria, transport protein evolution, and transposon-mediated directed mutations, while maintaining the Transporter Classification Database (TCDB).

labs.name: Policy Design and Evaluation Lab (PDEL)
labs.overview: PDEL is a research lab at UC San Diego's School of Global Policy and Strategy that conducts interdisciplinary research on policy design, evaluation, and implementation across various domains including environment, financial services, education, governance, public health, and trade.

labs.name: Institute for Genomic Medicine (IGM)
labs.overview: The Institute for Genomic Medicine aims to discover new pathways in human health and disease and translate these discoveries to clinical and preventive medicine through multidisciplinary research.

labs.name: UC San Diego Department of Radiology
labs.overview: The Department of Radiology conducts research on brain imaging, leveraging leading-edge technology to advance knowledge of brain function and disease states.

labs.name: Scripps Institution of Oceanography
labs.overview: A research institution dedicated to understanding and protecting the planet through oceanographic, earth science, and climate research across multiple disciplines.

labs.name: HXI - Human-centered eXtended Intelligence Research Lab
labs.overview: The HXI Research Lab focuses on the design, development, and evaluation of interactive technology spanning Ubiquitous Computing, Artificial Intelligence (AI), eXtended Reality (XR), and Immersive Visualization.

labs.name: School of Biological Sciences
labs.overview: The School of Biological Sciences at UC San Diego conducts research across multiple biological disciplines, including biochemistry, cell biology, ecology, evolution, genetics, neurobiology, and more.

labs.name: Kipps Research Lab
labs.overview: The Kipps Research Lab focuses on researching the immunobiology and genetics of human B-cell malignancies, with a particular emphasis on chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL).

labs.name: Zhao Lab
labs.overview: The Zhao Lab researches auxin biosynthesis and its role in plant development, focusing on molecular mechanisms of hormone production and signaling in plants.

labs.name: UCSD Neurosciences
labs.overview: A neuroscience research group focused on various neurological specialties including Alzheimer's disease, vascular neurology, child neurology, and neuromuscular medicine.

labs.name: Fierer Lab
labs.overview: The Fierer Lab explores the distribution and roles of microscopic organisms in diverse environments and the relevance of microbes to the health and function of ecosystems, plants, and animals (including humans).

labs.name: Jennings Lab
labs.overview: The lab studies protein folding, molecular biophysics, and the structural mechanisms of proteins like interleukin-1β, mitoNEET, and other signaling molecules.

labs.name: Nizet Lab
labs.overview: The Nizet lab investigates innate immunity, bacterial pathogenesis, therapeutics and vaccines against sepsis and multidrug resistant pathogens.

labs.name: The Schork Group
labs.overview: An energy market intelligence firm providing research, analysis, and strategic guidance for energy professionals and traders.

labs.name: Master of Advanced Studies in Precision Medicine Therapeutics in Oncology
labs.overview: An interdisciplinary graduate degree program focusing on personalized healthcare, leveraging genetics, data science, advanced medical technologies and therapies to tailor oncology treatments to individual patients.

labs.name: Fadare Lab
labs.overview: The Fadare Lab studies the fate and impacts of emerging environmental pollutants, using integrated analytical approaches and sustainable remediation technologies to understand pollutant behavior and develop innovative environmental protection strategies.

labs.name: Hui Lab
labs.overview: The Hui Lab studies the molecular mechanisms of immune checkpoint receptors, focusing on how these molecular 'brakes' control immune system responses and impact cancer immunotherapy.

labs.name: CARING Lab
labs.overview: The CARING (Community Action Research in Gerontology) Lab investigates social justice issues impacting older adults, people living with dementia, and caregivers, with a focus on advancing health equity in aging.

labs.name: Schlaepfer Lab
labs.overview: The lab focuses on understanding the molecular changes driving ovarian cancer anchorage-independent cell survival and treatment resistance.

labs.name: Lopez-Ramirez Lab
labs.overview: The lab investigates novel non-invasive therapies for cardiovascular and neurovascular diseases, focusing on cerebral cavernous malformations and neuroinflammation.

labs.name: The Reid Lab
labs.overview: The Reid Lab focuses on innovations at the interface of computational and synthetic organic chemistry, developing and applying machine learning algorithms for reaction and catalyst development.

labs.name: Chang Lab
labs.overview: The lab studies cellular and molecular mechanisms underlying T lymphocyte fate specification, focusing on how lymphocytes produce both terminally differentiated effector cells and self-renewing memory cells during immune responses.

labs.name: Gianneschi Group
labs.overview: The lab focuses on developing novel, stimuli-responsive supramolecular and nanoscale materials by combining synthetic organic and inorganic chemistry with biological molecules and polymers.

labs.name: Salk Institute for Biological Studies
labs.overview: The Salk Institute is an independent, nonprofit research institute focused on foundational research in areas like cancer, Alzheimer's disease, and agricultural challenges.

labs.name: Budin Lab
labs.overview: The Budin Lab uses interdisciplinary approaches to understand and engineer molecular processes on cell and organelle surfaces, focusing on lipid building blocks and membrane composition.

labs.name: Mechanotransduction in Immune Network Dynamics (MIND) Lab
labs.overview: Bridging mechanobiology, matrix viscoelasticity, biophysics, and immune cell functions for translational impact.

labs.name: Reck-Peterson Lab
labs.overview: The Reck-Peterson Lab studies the mechanisms of intracellular transport at molecular, cellular, and organismal scales, with research addressing fundamental cell biology questions relevant to human health and disease.

labs.name: Wael Al-Delaimy Research Group
labs.overview: A research group focused on multiple public health initiatives including community health workers, global environmental health, and global mental health.

labs.name: Susan Golden Lab
labs.overview: The lab studies circadian rhythms of gene expression in cyanobacteria, focusing on understanding the molecular mechanisms of biological clocks and metabolic engineering of cyanobacteria.

labs.name: Lipomi Research Group
labs.overview: A team of scientists and engineers interested in the overlap between energy, biomimetic materials and devices, and green chemistry and nanomanufacturing. They study fundamental and applied research relating molecular and nanoscale structure to function.

labs.name: Chien Lab
labs.overview: The lab develops computational tools for individualized diagnoses, treatment planning, and device evaluation based on analysis of patient medical images, with a focus on vascular diseases like brain aneurysms.

labs.name: George Lab Addiction Research Laboratory
labs.overview: The lab focuses on identifying the neurobiological mechanisms underlying drug addiction and developing novel pharmacological and non-pharmacological treatments to help people with substance use disorders.

labs.name: Toor Lab
labs.overview: The Toor lab focuses on investigating the structure and function of non-coding regions of prokaryotic and eukaryotic genomes, with particular interest in introns, retroelements, and RNA splicing evolution.

labs.name: Molina Lab
labs.overview: The Molina Lab focuses on understanding how mitochondrial function is related to aging and the development of age-related diseases, with research spanning biomarkers of aging, cognitive decline, and physical function.

labs.name: UC San Diego Division of Pulmonary, Critical Care, Sleep Medicine and Physiology
labs.overview: A research and clinical division focused on respiratory medicine, critical care, sleep medicine, and physiological research. The division provides clinical care and conducts research on lung diseases, sleep disorders, and critical care medicine.

labs.name: Chung Lab
labs.overview: An interdisciplinary research team devoted to developing and applying novel technologies for holistic understanding of large-scale complex biological systems, with a focus on brain function and dysfunction.

labs.name: Shi Lab (Laboratory of Optical Bioimaging and Spectroscopy)
labs.overview: The Shi Lab develops and applies advanced optical imaging technologies like stimulated Raman scattering (SRS) microscopy to study metabolic dynamics in aging and diseases at the subcellular level.

labs.name: Program in Materials Science and Engineering
labs.overview: The Program in Materials Science and Engineering at UC San Diego is an interdisciplinary program focused on research and education in materials science across multiple departments.

labs.name: Soft Active Materials Lab
labs.overview: The lab aims to understand the deformation and assembly of soft matter on the nano to macro-scales, and integrate their unique characteristics into new approaches for applications in biomedical devices, soft robotics, actuators, and sensors.

labs.name: Stelzer Lab
labs.overview: The Stelzer Lab focuses on the complex maternal neuroimmune adaptations during pregnancy, exploring interactions between the uterus and maternal brain during the peripartum period.

labs.name: Klemke Lab
labs.overview: The lab uses bioengineering methods to develop improved cell-based therapeutics to treat cancer and inflammatory diseases, covering areas including cancer biology, immunology, cell engineering, and signal transduction.

labs.name: Dorrestein Lab
labs.overview: The Dorrestein Lab develops and applies mass spectrometry methods for studying microbial communities in human, animal, and environmental systems, with a focus on understanding chemical interactions between microbes.

labs.name: Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute (HDSI)
labs.overview: The Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute aims to develop scientific foundations of data science, create new methods and infrastructure, and train students, faculty, and industry partners to solve complex global problems.

labs.name: Bioinformatics and Systems Biology PhD Program at UC San Diego
labs.overview: A graduate program focused on training students in computational and systems approaches to understanding biological systems through interdisciplinary research and education.

labs.name: Lab for Nano-bio-imaging and Devices
labs.overview: The lab focuses on developing integrated atomic force microscope (AFM) technologies to understand human diseases, design controlled drug delivery, and create chip-based biomarker sensors.

labs.name: Shiley Eye Institute
labs.overview: A comprehensive academic eye center focused on ophthalmology research, clinical care, and advanced treatments across all areas of eye care.

labs.name: Jared Young Lab
labs.overview: The Young Lab investigates the molecular biology and neural circuitry underlying behavioral abnormalities in major neuropsychiatric disorders, focusing on developing cross-species models to identify targetable mechanisms for mental illness treatments.

labs.name: Rangamani Group
labs.overview: A computational cellular mechanobiology lab broadly interested in the interplay of cell shape, signaling, and mechanics. They use principles of mechanics coupled with signal transduction to model and identify principles underlying biological phenomena.

labs.name: The Laurent Lab
labs.overview: The lab studies brain aging, focusing on understanding molecular mechanisms like alternative RNA splicing and chromatin structure regulation that maintain brain function and contribute to neurodegenerative diseases.

labs.name: Jacobs School of Engineering
labs.overview: The Jacobs School of Engineering is a top-ranked engineering school at UC San Diego that conducts research across six academic departments: Bioengineering, Computer Science and Engineering, Electrical and Computer Engineering, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering, Chemical and Nano Engineering, and Structural Engineering.

labs.name: IAS-USA (International Antiviral Society-USA)
labs.overview: The IAS-USA Core Faculty members are experts in the field of viral medicine, demonstrating excellence in teaching, clinical care, and commitment to the organization's mission.

labs.name: Troemel Lab
labs.overview: The Troemel Lab focuses on host/pathogen interactions, studying how pathogens attack their hosts and how hosts defend themselves, using the nematode Caenorhabditis elegans and its natural intracellular pathogens.

labs.name: Ghosh Lab
labs.overview: The Ghosh lab studies the cell biology of signal transduction, focusing on heterotrimeric G-proteins and their regulation by guanine-nucleotide exchange modulators (GEMs).

labs.name: Zhang Research Group
labs.overview: Our research focuses on creating innovative biomimetic nanotechnologies for biomedical applications such as countermeasures, drug delivery, vaccines, and diagnostics.
